Scenario.title: CostaRicto Communications Industry Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Communications Complex CostaRicto attackers target communications companies using spear phishing via compromised email to gain initial access.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Device theft", "Weak passwords", "Improper configuration", "Vulnerability", "Internet-facing assets", "Shared DB or directory", "Obsolete products", "Injection flaws", "Custom APIs", "Autonomous system numbers(ASNs)", "SSL Certificates and Attribution", "WHOIS records, contacts and history", "TCP/IP ports", "Public and private cloud", "Web server services such as emails and databases", "Shadow IT", "Poor implementation of access controls", "Malicious insiders", "Social engineering", "Insufficient physical security controls", "Authentication issues"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["External/Removable media", "Loss or Theft of equipment", "Attrition", "Web", "Account takeover", "Inadequate encryption", "Browser-based attack", "Application compromise", "Open port", "Vulnerability exploit", "Ransomware", "Malware", "Man in The Middle(MiTM)", "Session hijacking", "Brute-force attack", "Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)", "Watering-hole", "Compromised and weak credentials", "SIM Swap", "Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities", "Web forms", "IP address and IP blocks", "Domains and subdomains", "Botnet", "Credential compromise", "Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS)", "Replay Attack", "Cryptanalysis Attacks", "Side Channel Attacks", "Hardware Trojans", "Covert Attacks", "Sleep Deprivation Attack", "Clock Skewing", "Attacks Targeting System Reputation", "Data and Command Integrity Attack", "Eavesdropping", "Command and Control Attacks", "Phishing", "Impersonations/Spoofing", "Improper usage", "Dumpster diving", "Advanced Persistent Threat(APT)", "Baiting", "Shoulder surfing", "Reverse social engineering", "Business Email Compromise(BEC)", "Advanced Fee", "Extortion"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers use spear phishing via compromised email to trick employees into opening malicious attachments or clicking on links, allowing them to gain initial access to the company's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers utilize PowerShell for lateral movement and privilege escalation, executing malicious scripts to spread throughout the network.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers create scheduled tasks to maintain persistence on the compromised systems, ensuring continued access to the network.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to escalate privileges and gain further access to the network.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers obfuscate their malware to evade detection by security systems and maintain their presence on the network.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt brute force login attempts to gain credentials and further compromise the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan the network for vulnerable services to identify potential entry points for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers use remote services to move laterally within the network, spreading their presence and influence.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from compromised systems, further compromising the network.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate stolen data over alternative protocols, maintaining their covert presence.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt business operations through DoS attacks, causing significant damage to the company's reputation and finances.

Scenario.summary: The CostaRicto attackers employ a complex attack strategy targeting communications companies, utilizing spear phishing via compromised email to gain initial access, followed by execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
